Text:
One single continuous photograph — no panels, grids, or split frames.

Use the uploaded photo as the exact base composition. The street in the source
is empty — populate it with the people and vehicles specified below. Remove all
modern businesses, logos, brands, storefront names, banners, advertisements, and
signage visible in the source image.

PRESERVE (must match source exactly)
- camera: eye-level, street-facing, fov 75
- commercial arterial road, 4-lane, asphalt
- sidewalks present, curbs present
- driveways: north driveway apron, south driveway apron; parking: open asphalt apron surrounding pump islands
- gas station canopy building at center lot over pump islands, 1 story, steel frame, corrugated metal fascia, flat roof, 30 feet from road setback
- service station office building at rear right corner, 1 story, concrete block, brick veneer, flat parapet roof, at parking apron setback
- open service bay building at rear left corner, 1 story, concrete block, gabled metal roof, at parking apron setback
- utilities: overhead power lines on wooden poles, transformer on northeast corner pole
- landscape: flat asphalt apron, painted concrete curbing, narrow grass strip at road edge
- immutable elements: canopy with four corner support posts, pump island with eight dispensers, north and south driveway aprons
Keep this location instantly recognizable.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Create a TRUE 9:16 vertical portrait image — one single frame. Expand the SAME
scene naturally above (sky) and below (street/pavement) — this is canvas
extension of one photograph, not additional panels. Do not stretch or distort
the source geometry.

TRANSFORM TO 1995
Clinton era prosperity. A 1995 self-serve station — wide canopy over multiple islands, attached convenience store, the first pay-at-pump card readers.

PERIOD DETAILS
- ice merchandiser and stacked firewood bundles outside the store
- convenience store always attached
- price sign showing gas around $1.15
- main sign: an independent station sign in the style of Texaco
- window signs: '2 HOT DOGS $2', '32oz FOUNTAIN DRINK 89¢'
- a payphone mounted by the store entrance
- a wire rack of free auto-trader papers by the door
Typography: clean geometric sans-serif, early digital influence.
Sign text is only what appears in quotes — do not turn other words from this prompt into signage.

PEOPLE
EXACTLY 6 people, spread out with empty areas.
- a parent and kid walking into the store
- a woman swiping a card at the new pay-at-pump reader
- a paperboy with a canvas bag on a bike
Clothing: men in backwards caps, flannel shirts; women in high-waisted mom jeans, floral dresses. Fashion palette: forest green, maroon, mustard yellow. No posing or eye contact. All people on sidewalks, at storefronts, or beside parked vehicles — the driving lanes stay empty of pedestrians.

VEHICLES
EXACTLY 4 period vehicles, all different:
- 1990-1997 Mazda Miata — tiny rounded roadster, pop-up lights — navy blue
- 1994-1997 Honda Accord — smooth rounded sedan — black
- 1990-1994 Chevrolet Lumina — rounded mid-size sedan — hunter green
- 1992-1995 Pontiac Grand Am — compact with ribbed plastic cladding — teal
Parked with gaps; no vehicle newer than 1995.
PLACEMENT: the first two parked on the LEFT side; the last one driving away from the camera. No vehicle in the same spot as any other era.

ENVIRONMENT
- road markings: yellow center line, white edge lines, turn lane arrows
- utilities: overhead power lines standard, cable TV and early phone data lines on poles

TREES
- cottonwood tree at northeast corner of lot: very young sapling
- elm tree at sidewalk edge left of entrance: very young sapling
- cedar tree at back fence line: established tree, modest canopy
Tree sizes MUST follow this specification even where they differ from the source photo.

PHOTOGRAPHIC STYLE
COLOR photograph — Kodak Gold 200 or Fujifilm Superia 200 look.
Color: neutral to slightly warm tones. Grain: fine grain, cleaner than 1985.
Photorealistic — NOT black-and-white, no HDR.

PRIVACY
Blur all faces and license plates.
SelectedVehicles: 1990-1997 Mazda Miata — tiny rounded roadster, pop-up lights
1992-1995 Pontiac Grand Am — compact with ribbed plastic cladding
